description:
You are acting as a FinOps analyst who has just been asked to tighten the alerting thresholds for a small cloud-cost optimisation utility that lives inside the user’s home directory.

Current project structure (already present on disk):

/home/user/projects/cloud-cost-optimizer/
├── config/
│   ├── thresholds.yml          <– YAML file you must modify
│   └── alerts.toml             <– TOML file you must extend
└── logs/                       <– create the new log file here

1. YAML task  
   • File: /home/user/projects/cloud-cost-optimizer/config/thresholds.yml  
   • Replace the existing values with the new, optimised thresholds shown below.  
   • Preserve exact indentation (2 spaces) and key ordering (aws, azure, gcp).  

Desired final contents of thresholds.yml (YAML, no trailing spaces):
aws:
  monthly_budget: 5000
  alert_at_percent: 80
azure:
  monthly_budget: 3000
  alert_at_percent: 85
gcp:
  monthly_budget: 2000
  alert_at_percent: 75

2. TOML task  
   • File: /home/user/projects/cloud-cost-optimizer/config/alerts.toml  
   • Leave the existing [email] table untouched.  
   • Add a new [slack] table directly beneath it with **exactly** the following three key/value pairs and order.  

[slack]
channel = "#finops-alerts"
mention = "@channel"
enabled = true

3. Logging requirement  
   • Create /home/user/projects/cloud-cost-optimizer/logs/optimization.log (the logs directory already exists).  
   • The file must be plain text, tab-separated (TSV) **including the header** and terminated with a single trailing newline.  
   • Use the precise content below:

Provider	OldBudget	NewBudget	AlertPercent
AWS	7000	5000	80
Azure	5000	3000	85
GCP	4000	2000	75

Hints & expectations  
• A real analyst would need no more than 3-4 shell commands (e.g., using echo, printf, or sed).  
• When you finish, the automated tests will verify:  
  – The *exact* byte-for-byte contents of thresholds.yml, alerts.toml, and optimization.log.  
  – That optimization.log is located in /home/user/projects/cloud-cost-optimizer/logs/.  

Do not show the commands here; simply execute them in the terminal.
truth:
Pre-existing on disk before the task starts:

1. /home/user/projects/cloud-cost-optimizer/config/thresholds.yml
aws:
  monthly_budget: 7000
  alert_at_percent: 90
azure:
  monthly_budget: 5000
  alert_at_percent: 90
gcp:
  monthly_budget: 4000
  alert_at_percent: 90

2. /home/user/projects/cloud-cost-optimizer/config/alerts.toml
[email]
recipients = ["finops@example.com","cloudteam@example.com"]

3. /home/user/projects/cloud-cost-optimizer/logs/   (empty directory)

Ground-truth state after successful completion:

A. /home/user/projects/cloud-cost-optimizer/config/thresholds.yml (exact bytes)
aws:
  monthly_budget: 5000
  alert_at_percent: 80
azure:
  monthly_budget: 3000
  alert_at_percent: 85
gcp:
  monthly_budget: 2000
  alert_at_percent: 75
<EOF>

B. /home/user/projects/cloud-cost-optimizer/config/alerts.toml (exact bytes)
[email]
recipients = ["finops@example.com","cloudteam@example.com"]

[slack]
channel = "#finops-alerts"
mention = "@channel"
enabled = true
<EOF>

C. /home/user/projects/cloud-cost-optimizer/logs/optimization.log (exact bytes)
Provider	OldBudget	NewBudget	AlertPercent
AWS	7000	5000	80
Azure	5000	3000	85
GCP	4000	2000	75
<EOF>
name: task_000000_a8061493
